user_query:
I need a policy-compliant counter-brief I can lean on to push back against an investigative reporter’s draft without exposing anything we haven’t already placed on the record. Using only the local inputs provided, please do the following:

1) Read and synthesize:
- input/reporter_allegations.md (the reporter’s three core allegations)
- input/memos/talking_points.txt (candidate talking points; each line starts with a tag like [public], [internal], or [confidential])
- input/policy/disclosure_rules.yaml (disclosure policy: allowed tag, prohibited phrases, required disclaimer)
- input/policy/comms_config.py (communication settings: whether to include line numbers, max quote length, whether to include matched keywords)

2) Selection and compliance rules:
- Only select talking points from lines whose tag exactly matches the allowed_tag from input/policy/disclosure_rules.yaml.
- For each allegation, select up to two public talking points that are relevant by keyword overlap with the allegation text (case-insensitive, excluding common English stopwords such as "the, and, of, to, for"). Be transparent by listing the matched keywords if comms_config says to include them.
- Apply prohibited_phrases from input/policy/disclosure_rules.yaml by redacting any occurrence in chosen quotes with the literal token "[REDACTED]".
- Enforce MAX_QUOTE_CHARS and INCLUDE_SOURCE_LINE_NUMBERS from input/policy/comms_config.py when rendering quotes and citations.
- If no eligible public talking point exists for an allegation, note "No eligible public talking points found." for that item.

3) Deliverables (create both files):
- output/counter_brief.json with this structure:
  {
    "disclaimer": string (from required_disclaimer in YAML),
    "config": { "include_line_numbers": bool, "max_quote_chars": int, "include_matched_keywords": bool },
    "pairs": [
      {
        "allegation_id": integer order from the input file (1..n),
        "allegation_text": string,
        "matched_keywords": [strings] (include only if INCLUDE_MATCHED_KEYWORDS is True),
        "talking_points": [
          {
            "quote": string (redacted and truncated per rules),
            "source_file": "input/memos/talking_points.txt",
            "line_number": integer (include only if INCLUDE_SOURCE_LINE_NUMBERS is True)
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
- output/counter_brief.md including at the top the disclaimer from YAML, followed by a section for each allegation with its text, then up to two bullet-pointed, policy-compliant quotes (redacted/truncated as needed) and their source line numbers if enabled by comms_config.

Constraints:
- Do not invent or use any content beyond what’s in the provided files.
- All selected quotes must come from lines tagged with the allowed_tag in input/policy/disclosure_rules.yaml.
- Ensure no prohibited phrases appear unredacted in the outputs.
- Preserve the original wording of selected public lines except for redaction and length truncation per comms_config.

This must run locally using the given files only. Save outputs exactly to output/counter_brief.json and output/counter_brief.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
